steps.title: Map the passage’s two-part structure
steps.content: The first sentence names two specific buildings (ARK and House in Goido) as examples of a trend — juxtaposing metal with traditional organic materials. The second sentence explains where that trend came from: post-WWI emphasis on technological progress and longstanding aesthetic traditions. So the structure is: examples first, underlying principles second.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice against that structure
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘distinguishes two aesthetic approaches and argues one has more long-term impact’: The text never ranks or compares the two approaches by impact; it presents them as coexisting. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘names projects noteworthy for their materials and explains past important uses of those materials’: The second sentence explains historical principles driving the trend, not “past uses” of metal or wood. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘cites examples of a design trend and briefly establishes the principles underlying the trend’: Matches exactly — two buildings as examples, then the post-WWI and traditional-preservation principles behind them. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘introduces salient characteristics of two buildings and details the historical events that occasioned their designs’: Overstates the second sentence; the text gives broad historical context for a trend, not events that specifically caused these two buildings. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
